questions.prompt: You need to restore a backup file taken on a PBX running firmware 84.21.0.66 onto a customer's current Cloud PBX. The current PBX is on 84.19.0.110 and is Running. What happens?
choices: Restore succeeds; YCM handles firmware compatibility automatically.
Restore fails. The backup's firmware is higher than the target's, so YCM rejects it. Upgrade the target PBX firmware first or pick a different backup.
Restore succeeds but the PBX downgrades to match the backup.
Restore succeeds but only configuration data, not user data, migrates.
questions.explanation: Restore requires the backup firmware to be at or below the target PBX firmware. The file name encodes the firmware (PCE-Backup-{firmware}-{time}.bak) and YCM checks it before applying. Older backups onto newer PBXes work; the reverse doesn't.

questions.prompt: A customer reports 'our IVR sent the 4pm caller to the after-hours branch by mistake'. Where does this ticket get diagnosed?
choices: Stay in YCM; check the row and the alarm log.
Passwordless-login into PSE; open the CDR with Timeline View for that call, then read the time condition and the IVR configuration.
Resize the PBX; an IVR misroute means the customer is at capacity.
Restore from the most recent backup.
questions.explanation: Callflow surprises (time conditions, IVR routing) are PSE-side. YCM holds no per-callflow data. Descend, read the CDR for that specific call in Timeline View, then check the time condition's wall-clock range against the call's actual timestamp. PSE-triage course's pattern-3 covers this in detail.
